reasoning: The market estimate was 3% as of Oct 16. My searches through Oct 26, 2025 demonstrate that despite a rocky period earlier in the year regarding his sales performance, executive departures, and a WSJ report in May 2025 alleging a CEO search, Elon Musk and the Tesla board strongly denied he would step down. He even committed to staying 5 more years. By mid-October 2025, he was active on the Q3 earnings call as CEO safely securing his record-breaking compensation package. There is virtually no evidence to suggest he stepped down between Oct 16 and Oct 26. The base rate for a founder-CEO of a multi-hundred billion dollar company suddenly leaving within any given two-month window is roughly ~1-3%. Everything aligns closely with the market's 3% probability. I'll peg it slightly higher at 3.5% due to some non-zero background noise of previous executive departures and board friction in 2024/2025, but a resignation seems highly unlikely.
